description: Picture of all the people who attended the 2 day tour at OMRON's HQ
Picture of one of the OMRON specialists demoing their object recognition system. This is a specialized device that can quickly detect certain object's such as candy bars on a conveyor belt.
Arun and I arriving at OMRON's HQ. We were very excited for the sessions!
Picture of the table we arrived to for the sessions.
Arun and I showcasing the control panel we wired up during the first day. This was the same panel we wired for the WorldSkills finals but much neater!
Picture of the panel after it's finished building. This picture shows the light gates on the right.
Picture of the panel after it's finished building. This picture just shows the panel itself.
Safety light gates that were connected to the safety panel.
Traffic light sequence Arun and I made using one of OMRON's MAC. We were pretty jazzed about it!
Demostration cabinet for one of OMRON's MACs. This one is composed of the MAC, and some peripherals such as motors, lights, screens and buttons.
This is a video Arun and I made showcasing our hotel room. The room had a door that led to... Nowhere!

After finishing the first session day, Arun and I were hungry. We looked in google maps and found a Papa Jhons nearby. Turns out it was a restaurant, it was the headquarters for Papa Jhon himself! Would you believe that they do NOT serve pizza there!?
A video Arun and I took returning from Papa Jhon's HQ. We crossed the highway again which was not very fun, specially on an empty stomach.
This is a video of two of OMRON's robotic arms sorting random pieces on a conveyor belt.
Demo video of one of OMRON's mobile robots. Her name is Roxanne.
This is a video of Roxanne the robot mapping the room around us.
Video of Roxanne the robot playing a few fun sound effects. This was meant to showcase it's ability to warn staff members around it.
Roxanne the robot Imperial Marching around the room.
This is a video I took of the battery-less clicker system Omron has as a showcase. This has a VERY impressive range and it's meant to be used in factories as emergy shutdown buttons.
Video Arun and I took leaving OMRON's headquarters. What an amazing day it was!
Picture of some of the swag we got during the day. I still use the USB stick to this day!
Picture of Arun and I at a nearby restaurant we found after our Papa Jhon's plans were thwarted.
Picture of the demo conveyor belt OMRON had.
Video of one of OMRON's collaborative robotic arms picking and placing some boxes around.
Video of one of OMRON's heaby duty robots loading and unloading a box that was placed by the robotic arm.
Video of Roxanne the robot moving around.
Picture of Arun and I at the entrance of OMRON's Headquarters at Milton Keynes.
